title: Your Organs Are Aging at Different Rates.
summary: Proteomic aging clocks reveal that distinct organ systems age at different rates, predicting specific disease risks and overall longevity.

title: Lifespan-Extending Endogenous Metabolites
summary: Targeting metabolic networks with endogenous compounds offers a safer, more integrated strategy to delay aging by simultaneously modulating core hallmarks.

title: Diet vs. Exercise At the Molecular Level
summary: Calorie restriction and endurance exercise both reduce biological age, but through entirely distinct mechanisms—from microbiome remodeling to epigenetic reversal.

title: IL-11 Inhibition Extends Lifespan by 25%
summary: Targeting the pro-inflammatory cytokine IL-11 in aged mice reverses physical frailty, restores metabolic function, and extends median lifespan by 25%.

title: Immune Cells Engineered to Hunt Aging
summary: Repurposing CAR T-cell therapy to hunt and destroy 'zombie' senescent cells provides a long-lasting, vaccine-like shield against age-related decline.

title: The Unified Theory of Rejuvenation
summary: Three independent longevity interventions—Young Blood, Klotho, and Exercise—all converge on a single blood factor (PF4) to reverse brain aging.

title: The Information Theory of Aging
summary: Aging is a loss of epigenetic software, not a hardware mutation. And using OSK factors, this software can be rebooted.

title: The Pharmacological Holy Grail
summary: The landmark study proving that pharmacological inhibition of mTOR using Rapamycin extends maximum lifespan in mammals, even when started in old age.

title: Mitochondrial Rejuvenation
summary: The discovery of a microbiome-derived metabolite that triggers mitophagy, clearing dead mitochondria and extending lifespan.

title: The Communication Breakdown
summary: Declining NAD+ levels break the vital communication network between the nucleus and mitochondria, driving rapid cellular aging.

title: Small-Molecule Senolytics
summary: The discovery that a cheap, orally available cocktail of Dasatinib and Quercetin can hunt and destroy toxic zombie cells to extend lifespan.
